conflict: the night reveals what the daytime protocols conceal — alliances, prayers, fears, intimacies forming outside family lines
consequence: Leni discovers the Arash-Bett connection; the political crisis intrudes through distant sounds; the shrine at night is the inverse of the naming — truth without declaration
must_land_beats: Leni's insomnia walk through the shrine compound at night
Arash and Bett in the prayer courtyard — closer than the shrine's social rules allow, speaking as themselves rather than as family representatives
Peyam praying alone in the rain — his own prayers, his own language, his own god
Jin keeping vigil over sleeping Melie — his face in the lamplight
distant sounds: thunder or vehicles or gunfire — the political crisis as ambient threat
Leni writing in her journal: the most unguarded version of herself
